subject: Computer Networking
title: Network Attached Storage (NAS)
description: A fast breakdown of Network Attached Storage (NAS) for builders.

hook.text: A NAS is a dedicated storage box on your network. Any authorized device can read and write to it.

buildup.text: NAS devices use multiple drives in RAID arrays. RAID provides speed, redundancy, or both.

discovery.text: SMB and NFS let computers mount NAS drives as if they were local. File sharing becomes seamless.

twist.text: Enterprise NAS from NetApp and Synology can store petabytes with snapshots, replication, and encryption.

climax.text: For home users, a NAS replaces cloud storage. Your files, your network, no monthly fees.

punchline.text: A NAS puts shared storage on your network. Your own cloud.

quiz.question: What does RAID provide in a NAS device?
options: Data redundancy, speed improvement, or both
Automatic virus scanning of stored files
